articles.headline: Parliament Clears Landmark National Digital Infrastructure Act, Paving Way for Unified Digital Governance
articles.explainer:
• The Act aims to create a robust and secure digital public infrastructure, encompassing digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
• It seeks to standardize digital services across government and private sectors, promoting interoperability and efficiency.
• Key provisions include a new Digital India Authority for oversight and a framework for data protection and cyber security.
• The legislation is expected to accelerate India's digital economy growth and enhance citizen services delivery.
• Concerns have been raised regarding potential data monopolies and the balance between innovation and regulation.
gsSubTopics: Governance, E-governance
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
IT & Computers
Cyber Security
prelimsPoints: The National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) is a new legislation passed by Parliament in 2025.
It aims to create a unified framework for digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
A new 'Digital India Authority' is proposed for oversight.
It emphasizes data protection and cyber security measures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of NDIA for India's digital economy and governance.
Analyze the challenges in implementation, including data privacy, federal cooperation, and technological obsolescence.
Evaluate its impact on the 'Digital India' initiative and achieving inclusive growth.
Examine how it balances innovation with regulatory oversight and consumer protection.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital network with interconnected nodes representing various services and data flow.
articles.trivia: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is often cited as a successful example of public digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Former Meghalaya CM D. D. Lapang Passes Away at 91, Leaves Legacy of Regional Politics
articles.explainer:
• D. D. Lapang, a veteran politician, served four terms as the Chief Minister of Meghalaya.
• He was a prominent figure in North-Eastern politics, known for his consensus-building approach.
• Lapang was instrumental in navigating complex coalition politics in the state.
• He belonged to the Indian National Congress for most of his political career.
• His demise marks the end of an era in Meghalaya's political landscape.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: D. D. Lapang served as the Chief Minister of Meghalaya.
He held the CM's office for four terms.
He was associated with the Indian National Congress.
Meghalaya is a state in North-Eastern India.
mainsPoints: The role of regional leaders in maintaining political stability in North-Eastern states.
Challenges of coalition governments in states with diverse ethnic compositions.
articles.imageDescription: File photo of D. D. Lapang addressing a gathering.
articles.trivia: Meghalaya, meaning 'abode of clouds', was carved out of Assam in 1972.

articles.headline: India Unveils 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0' to Boost Marine Resource Sustainability and Economic Growth
articles.explainer:
• The second phase of the mission focuses on advanced marine technology, sustainable fishing practices, and ocean energy.
• It aims to create new economic opportunities for coastal communities and promote responsible tourism.
• Key pillars include deep-sea exploration, marine spatial planning, and climate change adaptation for coastal regions.
• The mission emphasizes scientific research and indigenous technology development for ocean resources.
• It aligns with India's commitments to UN Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications
prelimsPoints: Blue Economy Mission 2.0 is an initiative by the Government of India.
Its primary goals are sustainable use of marine resources and economic growth.
Focus areas include deep-sea exploration, sustainable fishing, and ocean energy.
It is linked to UN Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Blue Economy for India's economic development and environmental sustainability.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a mission, including technological gaps, regulatory hurdles, and stakeholder conflicts.
Evaluate its potential impact on coastal livelihoods, national security, and India's geopolitical standing in the Indian Ocean region.
Suggest measures for effective inter-ministerial coordination and community participation.
articles.imageDescription: An infographic showing various aspects of the Blue Economy, including fisheries, shipping, and renewable ocean energy.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km and an Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of more than 2 million sq km.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Economic Headwinds and Domestic Inflationary Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach due to persistent global economic uncertainties and elevated core inflation.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were assessed as adequate, but vigilance remains high.
• Experts suggest a wait-and-watch approach by the central bank before considering any rate cuts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate was maintained at 6.5% by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
India's inflation target is 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Global economic headwinds and domestic inflationary pressures influenced the decision.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the potential impact of a prolonged status quo on interest rates on investment, consumption, and credit growth.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India's monetary policy framework and its effectiveness.
Evaluate how external factors like global crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions influence domestic monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: Graph showing the trend of RBI's repo rate over the past year.
articles.trivia: The MPC in India consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

articles.headline: India and Japan Bolster Strategic Partnership with New Defence and Technology Cooperation Accords
articles.explainer:
• The two nations signed agreements focusing on joint development of advanced defence technologies and military exercises.
• Cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including AI and semiconductors, was also a key agenda.
• The partnership aims to enhance regional stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both countries emphasized shared democratic values and a commitment to a rules-based international order.
• The agreements build upon the existing 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' between India and Japan.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan signed new agreements on defence and technology cooperation.
The partnership aims to enhance security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Areas of cooperation include AI, semiconductors, and joint military exercises.
Both nations are members of the QUAD grouping.
mainsPoints: Examine the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how enhanced defence and technology cooperation contributes to regional security and stability.
Analyze the economic implications of this partnership, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India-Japan relations in fostering a rules-based international order.
articles.imageDescription: Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during a bilateral meeting.
articles.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India holds a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08' for Enhanced Agricultural and Disaster Monitoring
articles.explainer:
• The EOS-08 satellite was launched using the PSLV-C58 from Sriharikota, marking another milestone for ISRO.
• It is designed to provide high-resolution images for agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster management.
• The satellite features enhanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) capabilities for all-weather imaging.
• Data from EOS-08 will be crucial for natural resource management and environmental monitoring.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: EOS-08 is an Earth Observation Satellite launched by ISRO.
It was launched using the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58).
Its applications include agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster monitoring.
The satellite features Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) for all-weather imaging.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of India's space program in national development, with a focus on Earth observation satellites.
Analyze how advanced remote sensing technologies, like SAR, contribute to disaster management and resource planning.
Examine the potential for commercialization of ISRO's services and its implications for India's space economy.
Evaluate the significance of indigenous space technology in achieving self-reliance and strategic autonomy.
articles.imageDescription: Launch of PSLV-C58 carrying EOS-08 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
articles.trivia: India is one of the few countries capable of launching satellites using its own launch vehicles.

articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Skill Development Policy 2025' to Create a Future-Ready Workforce
articles.explainer:
• The new policy aims to align skill development programs with industry 4.0 requirements and emerging job markets.
• It emphasizes digital literacy, green skills, and entrepreneurship training for youth.
• A focus on reskilling and upskilling the existing workforce is a key component.
• The policy promotes greater private sector participation and industry-academia collaboration.
• It seeks to formalize informal skills and ensure greater portability of certifications across states.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
articles.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: The National Skill Development Policy 2025 focuses on aligning skills with Industry 4.0.
It promotes digital literacy, green skills, and entrepreneurship.
Reskilling and upskilling of the existing workforce are core objectives.
The policy seeks increased private sector and industry-academia collaboration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for skill development policies in India to harness its demographic dividend.
Analyze how the 'National Skill Development Policy 2025' addresses the challenges of unemployment and underemployment.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and industry collaboration in achieving the policy's objectives.
Examine the importance of 'green skills' and 'digital literacy' in preparing the Indian workforce for future challenges.
articles.imageDescription: Students participating in a vocational training program.
articles.trivia: India is projected to have the largest working-age population in the world by 2030.

articles.headline: New Framework for Cyber Security Collaboration Between States and Central Agencies Announced
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs launched a new framework to enhance coordination in combating cybercrime and cyber threats.
• It establishes a unified reporting mechanism and incident response protocols for state and central agencies.
• The framework aims to share intelligence, best practices, and resources more effectively.
• Capacity building for state law enforcement in cyber forensics and investigation is a key focus.
• This initiative is critical to address the rising sophistication and cross-border nature of cyber attacks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: A new framework for cyber security collaboration has been launched by MHA.
It aims to improve coordination between central and state agencies in combating cybercrime.
Key aspects include unified reporting, incident response, and intelligence sharing.
Capacity building for state law enforcement in cyber forensics is a focus.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and the necessity for robust inter-agency coordination.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the new framework in addressing the challenges of cyber security.
Examine the role of technology and human resources in strengthening India's cyber defense capabilities.
Evaluate the implications of cyber security breaches on national security, critical infrastructure, and individual privacy.
articles.imageDescription: A network security graphic illustrating data protection and threat detection.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyber attacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to internal security through communication networks

articles.headline: Centre Announces Special Infrastructure Development Package for North-Eastern States
articles.explainer:
• The new package focuses on enhancing connectivity, digital infrastructure, and renewable energy projects.
• It aims to accelerate socio-economic development and integrate the region further with mainstream India.
• Special emphasis is laid on improving road, rail, and air links to boost trade and tourism.
• Funding will also be directed towards skill development and promoting local entrepreneurship.
• This initiative aligns with India's 'Act East Policy' and strengthens federal cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER)
prelimsPoints: A special infrastructure development package was announced for North-Eastern states.
The package targets connectivity, digital infrastructure, and renewable energy.
It aligns with India's 'Act East Policy'.
The Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER) is involved.
mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent developmental challenges faced by India's North-Eastern states and the rationale behind special packages.
Analyze how enhanced infrastructure and connectivity can transform the region's economy and social fabric.
Evaluate the role of the 'Act East Policy' in integrating the North-East with Southeast Asian economies.
Examine the importance of cultural preservation and environmental sustainability in the region's development initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Map of North-Eastern India highlighting new infrastructure projects.
articles.trivia: The North-Eastern Region (NER) consists of eight states: Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, and Tripura.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure

articles.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Ambit of Free Speech in Digital Media, Upholds Reasonable Restrictions
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized that freedom of speech on digital platforms is not absolute.
• It reiterated that reasonable restrictions under Article 19(2) of the Constitution apply equally to online content.
• The judgment provided guidelines for distinguishing between legitimate criticism, hate speech, and defamation in the digital realm.
• It called for a balanced approach to protect fundamental rights while preventing misuse of digital platforms.
• The ruling has significant implications for social media intermediaries and content creators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court ruled on freedom of speech in digital media.
It affirmed that reasonable restrictions under Article 19(2) apply to online content.
The ruling aims to distinguish between legitimate criticism, hate speech, and defamation.
Article 19(1)(a) guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
mainsPoints: Discuss the complexities of balancing freedom of speech with public order, national security, and individual rights in the digital age.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling for content regulation on social media platforms and accountability of intermediaries.
Examine how this judgment contributes to the ongoing debate on digital rights and responsibilities in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions in the context of rapidly evolving technology.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books on a judicial desk, symbolizing a court ruling.
articles.trivia: The 'reasonable restrictions' under Article 19(2) include grounds like public order, decency, morality, and defamation.

articles.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh, Offering New Insights into Silk Route History
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a large complex of monastic structures.
• Preliminary findings suggest the monastery dates back to the 8th-9th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence.
• Artifacts like pottery, sculptures, and ancient manuscripts were recovered from the site.
• The discovery highlights Ladakh's historical significance as a crucial hub on the ancient Silk Route.
• Further excavations are expected to shed more light on the cultural exchange and trade networks of the era.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Ancient Buddhist monastery remains were unearthed by ASI in Ladakh.
The site dates back to the 8th-9th century CE.
Artifacts found include pottery, sculptures, and manuscripts.
The discovery emphasizes Ladakh's role on the ancient Silk Route.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the role of Ladakh as a cultural and trade bridge between India, Central Asia, and Tibet via the Silk Route.
Examine the challenges in preserving historical sites in high-altitude regions like Ladakh.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site in Ladakh revealing ancient stone structures.
articles.trivia: The Silk Route was an ancient network of trade routes that connected the East and West, primarily for silk trade but also for cultural exchange.

articles.headline: National Mission on Sustainable Water Management Releases Comprehensive Action Plan for Water Security
articles.explainer:
• The mission's action plan focuses on integrated water resource management, emphasizing both demand and supply-side interventions.
• Key strategies include promoting water-efficient agriculture, rainwater harvesting, and groundwater recharge.
• It also addresses urban water supply, wastewater treatment, and rejuvenation of traditional water bodies.
• The plan calls for enhanced inter-state cooperation and community participation in water governance.
• Climate change impacts on water resources and adaptation strategies are central to the mission's goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: The National Mission on Sustainable Water Management released its action plan.
It focuses on integrated water resource management.
Strategies include water-efficient agriculture, rainwater harvesting, and groundwater recharge.
It addresses urban water supply and wastewater treatment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted water crisis in India and the need for a comprehensive national action plan.
Analyze the effectiveness of integrated water resource management approaches in addressing both rural and urban water challenges.
Evaluate the role of inter-state cooperation and community participation in ensuring water security.
Examine the impact of climate change on India's water resources and suggest adaptation and mitigation strategies.
articles.imageDescription: A diagram illustrating different methods of water harvesting and conservation.
articles.trivia: India holds 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Incubation Fund to Boost Agri-Tech Startups and Modernize Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The 'Krishi Navachar Nidhi' (Agricultural Innovation Fund) aims to support startups developing tech solutions for agriculture.
• Focus areas include precision farming, supply chain management, farm mechanization, and market linkages.
• The fund provides financial assistance, mentorship, and access to research facilities for selected startups.
• It seeks to bridge the gap between agricultural research and practical application on the ground.
• This initiative is expected to enhance farmer income, improve productivity, and reduce post-harvest losses.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The 'Krishi Navachar Nidhi' is a new incubation fund for agri-tech startups.
It supports startups in areas like precision farming and supply chain management.
The fund provides financial assistance and mentorship.
Its objective is to modernize agriculture and enhance farmer income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of technology and innovation in transforming India's agricultural sector and addressing its challenges.
Analyze how government support for agri-tech startups can contribute to improving farmer incomes and food security.
Evaluate the potential of precision farming and digital agriculture in enhancing productivity and sustainability.
Examine the hurdles faced by agri-tech startups in India and suggest policy measures to foster their growth.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers using drones for crop monitoring and smart irrigation systems.
articles.trivia: India's agricultural sector employs more than half of the country's workforce.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: e-technology in the aid of farmers

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Access to Climate Finance and Technology Transfer at UN General Assembly
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for developed nations to fulfill their commitments on climate finance to developing countries.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for accessible and affordable climate technologies for mitigation and adaptation.
• It highlighted the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• India underscored its own ambitious climate action targets despite limited historical emissions.
• The discussions focused on accelerating global climate action ahead of the next COP summit.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: India advocated for equitable climate finance and technology transfer at the UNGA.
It emphasized the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
Developed nations' commitments on climate finance were a key point.
The discussions are crucial for the upcoming Conference of Parties (COP) summit.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on climate justice and its role in advocating for developing countries' interests in global climate negotiations.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and accessing adequate climate finance and technology transfer for developing nations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international mechanisms and agreements in addressing climate change, considering the principle of CBDR-RC.
Examine India's own climate action initiatives and their alignment with global sustainability goals.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from various countries attending a session at the UN General Assembly.
articles.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

articles.headline: Ministry of Civil Aviation Releases Draft Policy for Urban Air Mobility and Drone-based Logistics
articles.explainer:
• The draft policy aims to create a regulatory framework for electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
• It outlines guidelines for drone operations in urban areas, including delivery services and passenger transport.
• Safety, security, and air traffic management for urban air mobility are key considerations.
• The policy encourages investment and innovation in the nascent urban air mobility sector.
• It seeks to alleviate urban congestion and enhance last-mile connectivity through advanced aerial solutions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Civil Aviation released a draft policy for Urban Air Mobility (UAM).
It includes regulations for eVTOL aircraft and drone-based logistics.
Safety, security, and air traffic management are key focus areas.
The policy aims to reduce urban congestion and improve connectivity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Urban Air Mobility (UAM) and drone technology to revolutionize transportation and logistics in India.
Analyze the regulatory challenges involved in integrating UAM into existing urban infrastructure and airspace management.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and environmental impacts of drone-based services and eVTOL aircraft.
Examine the role of government policy in fostering innovation while ensuring safety and public acceptance of new aerial technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Concept art of an eVTOL aircraft flying over a futuristic city skyline.
articles.trivia: India's Drone Rules 2021 liberalized regulations for drone operations in the country.

articles.headline: G20 Sherpas Meet to Review Progress on India's Presidency Legacy Projects, Focus on Global South Development
articles.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened to assess the implementation of key initiatives launched during India's presidency in 2023.
• Discussions focused on the 'Voice of Global South Summit' outcomes and strengthening multilateral development banks.
• Progress on digital public infrastructure, climate finance, and sustainable development goals was reviewed.
• India emphasized the need for continued global cooperation to address challenges faced by developing countries.
• The meeting aimed to ensure continuity and impact of India's G20 legacy beyond its immediate term.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 Sherpas met to review progress on India's presidency legacy projects.
Key focus areas included outcomes from the 'Voice of Global South Summit'.
Discussions covered digital public infrastructure and climate finance.
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's G20 Presidency and its efforts to amplify the voice of the Global South.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring the continuity and effective implementation of G20 legacy projects across different presidencies.
Evaluate the role of multilateral institutions in addressing global challenges such as climate change, debt distress, and sustainable development.
Examine India's growing influence in global governance and its commitment to multilateralism.
articles.imageDescription: Official logo of India's G20 Presidency with delegates in a meeting.
articles.trivia: The G20 represents around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

articles.headline: National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 Targets Zero Landfill Cities by 2035
articles.explainer:
• The new policy aims to achieve 100% waste segregation at source and promote circular economy principles.
• It mandates advanced waste processing technologies, including Waste-to-Energy (WtE) plants and composting.
• Financial incentives and penalties are introduced to ensure compliance by urban local bodies and citizens.
• The policy emphasizes public awareness campaigns and private sector investment in waste management infrastructure.
• It is designed to tackle the growing urban waste crisis and improve public health and environmental quality.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
articles.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 targets zero landfill cities by 2035.
It mandates 100% waste segregation at source.
The policy promotes Waste-to-Energy (WtE) plants and composting.
Financial incentives and penalties are part of the policy framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the magnitude of India's urban waste crisis and its environmental and public health implications.
Analyze the key features of the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 and its potential for transforming waste management practices.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving zero landfill cities, including behavioral change, technological adoption, and financial viability.
Examine the role of local governments, private sector, and citizen participation in successful policy implementation.
articles.imageDescription: A modern waste processing facility with waste segregation and recycling operations.
articles.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

articles.headline: India-Africa Trade Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Partnerships and Capacity Building
articles.explainer:
• The annual summit aimed at strengthening economic ties and strategic cooperation between India and African nations.
• Key agreements were signed for digital infrastructure development, fintech collaboration, and skill enhancement programs.
• India reiterated its commitment to supporting Africa's development priorities through concessional credit lines and technical assistance.
• Discussions also covered food security, healthcare, and renewable energy partnerships.
• The summit emphasized a 'South-South Cooperation' model based on mutual respect and shared growth.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Trade Summit focused on digital partnerships and capacity building.
Agreements were signed for digital infrastructure and fintech collaboration.
India offers concessional credit lines and technical assistance to African nations.
The summit promotes a 'South-South Cooperation' model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations and the significance of 'South-South Cooperation' in a multipolar world.
Analyze the key areas of economic engagement between India and African nations, particularly in digital and capacity building sectors.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic and economic footprint across Africa.
Examine how India's development partnership model differs from that of other global powers in Africa.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and African delegates at a conference table with flags of participating nations.
articles.trivia: India is Africa's third-largest trading partner, and Africa is India's fourth-largest trading partner.

articles.headline: India's Renewable Energy Progress Report Shows Significant Strides Towards 2030 Targets
articles.explainer:
• The report highlights a substantial increase in solar and wind power generation capacity over the last year.
• It notes accelerated deployment of utility-scale renewable projects and decentralized solutions.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition for large projects.
• India is on track to achieve its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The report calls for continued policy support and private investment to meet ambitious 2030 targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy capacity has seen significant growth.
The country is progressing towards its 2030 Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
Challenges include grid integration and energy storage solutions.
Solar and wind power are major contributors to renewable energy generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's journey towards achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets and its contribution to global climate action.
Analyze the key drivers and challenges in accelerating the deployment of renewable energy technologies in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies, private investment, and technological innovation in the renewable energy sector.
Examine the implications of a growing renewable energy share on India's energy security and economic development.
articles.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching across a landscape under a clear sky.
articles.trivia: India has the world's fourth-largest installed renewable energy capacity.

highlights: Parliament passes landmark National Digital Infrastructure Act for unified digital governance and economic growth.
Former Meghalaya CM D. D. Lapang, a veteran regional politician, passes away at 91.
India launches 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0' to boost sustainable marine resource utilization and coastal development.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate, citing global economic uncertainty and domestic inflationary pressures.
India and Japan deepen strategic ties with new agreements on defence and critical technology cooperation.
ISRO successfully launches advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08' for enhanced monitoring capabilities.
Government unveils 'National Skill Development Policy 2025' to create a future-ready workforce aligned with Industry 4.0.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) 2025, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to establish a unified framework for digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
2. It proposes the creation of a 'Digital India Authority' for regulatory oversight.
3. The Act primarily focuses on safeguarding critical national infrastructure from cyber attacks, excluding data privacy.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 and 2 are correct. The NDIA aims to create a robust and secure digital public infrastructure and proposes a 'Digital India Authority' for oversight. Statement 3 is incorrect as the Act explicitly includes data protection and cyber security, not excluding data privacy.

prelimsMocks.question: D. D. Lapang, who recently passed away, was a prominent politician associated with which of the following states?
prelimsMocks.explanation: D. D. Lapang served four terms as the Chief Minister of Meghalaya, making him a significant political figure in the state.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following best describes the primary objective of India's 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0'?
options: (a) To establish India as a dominant naval power in the Indian Ocean.
(b) To promote sustainable utilization of marine resources for economic growth and environmental protection.
(c) To focus exclusively on deep-sea mining for rare earth elements.
(d) To develop advanced submarine technology for coastal defence.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To promote sustainable utilization of marine resources for economic growth and environmental protection.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0' aims for advanced marine technology, sustainable fishing practices, ocean energy, and deep-sea exploration, all under the umbrella of sustainable and responsible use of ocean resources.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC):
1. The MPC consists of six members, with three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.
2. The primary objective of the MPC is to achieve price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The MPC's decisions are binding on the Central Government.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The MPC has six members (3 RBI, 3 external) and its primary objective is price stability with growth. Statement 3 is incorrect; while the MPC's decisions on policy rates are binding on the RBI, they are not binding on the Central Government.

prelimsMocks.question: EOS-08, recently launched by ISRO, is primarily an Earth Observation Satellite designed for which of the following applications?
options: (c) Agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster management.
(d) Navigational services for global positioning systems.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster management.
prelimsMocks.explanation: EOS-08 is an advanced Earth Observation Satellite with high-resolution imaging capabilities, specifically designed to provide data for agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster management, utilizing Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR).

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the transformative potential of the National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) in achieving India's vision of a digitally empowered society and a knowledge economy. What are the associated challenges in balancing innovation, data privacy, and federal cooperation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Governance, E-governance; GS3: IT & Computers, Cyber Security
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NDIA's objectives. Detail its potential impact on governance, economy, and citizen services. Then, critically analyze challenges like data privacy concerns, the need for robust cyber security, ensuring federal coordination, and preventing monopolistic tendencies. Conclude with a balanced outlook on its implementation.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze India's 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0' in the context of sustainable development goals and its implications for coastal communities, marine biodiversity, and India's geopolitical standing in the Indian Ocean region.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Indian Economy; GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements
mainsMocks.approach: Define Blue Economy and the mission's objectives. Discuss its alignment with SDGs (especially SDG 14). Analyze its positive implications for coastal livelihoods, sustainable resource extraction, and marine conservation. Also, address potential geopolitical implications and challenges in implementation, such as technological gaps and environmental risks. Conclude with recommendations for effective governance.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the evolving strategic partnership between India and Japan, highlighting its significance for Indo-Pacific security and economic cooperation in a multipolar world. What are the key areas of convergence and divergence that shape this relationship?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership'. Discuss convergence in Indo-Pacific vision, defence cooperation, and critical technologies. Analyze economic cooperation, including infrastructure projects. Address potential areas of divergence or challenges. Conclude by emphasizing the importance of this partnership for regional stability and India's foreign policy.

quickBytes.text: The Repo Rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
D. D. Lapang served as Chief Minister of Meghalaya for four non-consecutive terms.
EOS-08 is an Earth Observation Satellite launched by ISRO, equipped with Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR).
The 'Blue Economy' emphasizes sustainable use of ocean resources for economic growth and improved livelihoods.
Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and heritage preservation.
The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
The National Skill Development Policy aims to align workforce skills with Industry 4.0 requirements.
Ladakh is strategically located on the ancient Silk Route, facilitating cultural and trade exchanges.
Climate finance refers to funding from public and private sources for climate change mitigation and adaptation.

GS1.headline: ASI Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery Remains in Ladakh, Offering New Insights into Silk Route History
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a large complex of monastic structures.
• Preliminary findings suggest the monastery dates back to the 8th-9th century CE, indicating early Buddhist presence.
• Artifacts like pottery, sculptures, and ancient manuscripts were recovered from the site.
• The discovery highlights Ladakh's historical significance as a crucial hub on the ancient Silk Route.
• Further excavations are expected to shed more light on the cultural exchange and trade networks of the era.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: Ancient Buddhist monastery remains were unearthed by ASI in Ladakh.
The site dates back to the 8th-9th century CE.
Artifacts found include pottery, sculptures, and manuscripts.
The discovery emphasizes Ladakh's role on the ancient Silk Route.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of archaeological discoveries in reconstructing India's ancient history and cultural heritage.
Analyze the role of Ladakh as a cultural and trade bridge between India, Central Asia, and Tibet via the Silk Route.
Examine the challenges in preserving historical sites in high-altitude regions like Ladakh.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeological excavation site in Ladakh revealing ancient stone structures.
GS1.trivia: The Silk Route was an ancient network of trade routes that connected the East and West, primarily for silk trade but also for cultural exchange.

GS2.headline: Parliament Clears Landmark National Digital Infrastructure Act, Paving Way for Unified Digital Governance
GS2.explainer:
• The Act aims to create a robust and secure digital public infrastructure, encompassing digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
• It seeks to standardize digital services across government and private sectors, promoting interoperability and efficiency.
• Key provisions include a new Digital India Authority for oversight and a framework for data protection and cyber security.
• The legislation is expected to accelerate India's digital economy growth and enhance citizen services delivery.
• Concerns have been raised regarding potential data monopolies and the balance between innovation and regulation.
gsSubTopics: Governance, E-governance
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
IT & Computers
Cyber Security
prelimsPoints: The National Digital Infrastructure Act (NDIA) is a new legislation passed by Parliament in 2025.
It aims to create a unified framework for digital identity, payments, and data exchange.
A new 'Digital India Authority' is proposed for oversight.
It emphasizes data protection and cyber security measures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of NDIA for India's digital economy and governance.
Analyze the challenges in implementation, including data privacy, federal cooperation, and technological obsolescence.
Evaluate its impact on the 'Digital India' initiative and achieving inclusive growth.
Examine how it balances innovation with regulatory oversight and consumer protection.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital network with interconnected nodes representing various services and data flow.
GS2.trivia: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) is often cited as a successful example of public digital infrastructure.

GS2.headline: Former Meghalaya CM D. D. Lapang Passes Away at 91, Leaves Legacy of Regional Politics
GS2.explainer:
• D. D. Lapang, a veteran politician, served four terms as the Chief Minister of Meghalaya.
• He was a prominent figure in North-Eastern politics, known for his consensus-building approach.
• Lapang was instrumental in navigating complex coalition politics in the state.
• He belonged to the Indian National Congress for most of his political career.
• His demise marks the end of an era in Meghalaya's political landscape.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
prelimsPoints: D. D. Lapang served as the Chief Minister of Meghalaya.
He held the CM's office for four terms.
He was associated with the Indian National Congress.
Meghalaya is a state in North-Eastern India.
mainsPoints: The role of regional leaders in maintaining political stability in North-Eastern states.
Challenges of coalition governments in states with diverse ethnic compositions.
GS2.imageDescription: File photo of D. D. Lapang addressing a gathering.
GS2.trivia: Meghalaya, meaning 'abode of clouds', was carved out of Assam in 1972.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Bolster Strategic Partnership with New Defence and Technology Cooperation Accords
GS2.explainer:
• The two nations signed agreements focusing on joint development of advanced defence technologies and military exercises.
• Cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, including AI and semiconductors, was also a key agenda.
• The partnership aims to enhance regional stability and security in the Indo-Pacific region.
• Both countries emphasized shared democratic values and a commitment to a rules-based international order.
• The agreements build upon the existing 'Special Strategic and Global Partnership' between India and Japan.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India and Japan signed new agreements on defence and technology cooperation.
The partnership aims to enhance security in the Indo-Pacific region.
Areas of cooperation include AI, semiconductors, and joint military exercises.
Both nations are members of the QUAD grouping.
mainsPoints: Examine the strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indo-Pacific.
Discuss how enhanced defence and technology cooperation contributes to regional security and stability.
Analyze the economic implications of this partnership, particularly in critical and emerging technologies.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India-Japan relations in fostering a rules-based international order.
GS2.imageDescription: Prime Ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during a bilateral meeting.
GS2.trivia: Japan is one of the few countries with which India holds a '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers).

GS2.headline: Government Unveils 'National Skill Development Policy 2025' to Create a Future-Ready Workforce
GS2.explainer:
• The new policy aims to align skill development programs with industry 4.0 requirements and emerging job markets.
• It emphasizes digital literacy, green skills, and entrepreneurship training for youth.
• A focus on reskilling and upskilling the existing workforce is a key component.
• The policy promotes greater private sector participation and industry-academia collaboration.
• It seeks to formalize informal skills and ensure greater portability of certifications across states.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry- the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
Human Resources
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
GS2.source: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
prelimsPoints: The National Skill Development Policy 2025 focuses on aligning skills with Industry 4.0.
It promotes digital literacy, green skills, and entrepreneurship.
Reskilling and upskilling of the existing workforce are core objectives.
The policy seeks increased private sector and industry-academia collaboration.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical need for skill development policies in India to harness its demographic dividend.
Analyze how the 'National Skill Development Policy 2025' addresses the challenges of unemployment and underemployment.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and industry collaboration in achieving the policy's objectives.
Examine the importance of 'green skills' and 'digital literacy' in preparing the Indian workforce for future challenges.
GS2.imageDescription: Students participating in a vocational training program.
GS2.trivia: India is projected to have the largest working-age population in the world by 2030.

GS2.headline: Centre Announces Special Infrastructure Development Package for North-Eastern States
GS2.explainer:
• The new package focuses on enhancing connectivity, digital infrastructure, and renewable energy projects.
• It aims to accelerate socio-economic development and integrate the region further with mainstream India.
• Special emphasis is laid on improving road, rail, and air links to boost trade and tourism.
• Funding will also be directed towards skill development and promoting local entrepreneurship.
• This initiative aligns with India's 'Act East Policy' and strengthens federal cooperation.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS2.source: Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER)
prelimsPoints: A special infrastructure development package was announced for North-Eastern states.
The package targets connectivity, digital infrastructure, and renewable energy.
It aligns with India's 'Act East Policy'.
The Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER) is involved.
mainsPoints: Discuss the persistent developmental challenges faced by India's North-Eastern states and the rationale behind special packages.
Analyze how enhanced infrastructure and connectivity can transform the region's economy and social fabric.
Evaluate the role of the 'Act East Policy' in integrating the North-East with Southeast Asian economies.
Examine the importance of cultural preservation and environmental sustainability in the region's development initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of North-Eastern India highlighting new infrastructure projects.
GS2.trivia: The North-Eastern Region (NER) consists of eight states: Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim, and Tripura.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Clarifies Ambit of Free Speech in Digital Media, Upholds Reasonable Restrictions
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, emphasized that freedom of speech on digital platforms is not absolute.
• It reiterated that reasonable restrictions under Article 19(2) of the Constitution apply equally to online content.
• The judgment provided guidelines for distinguishing between legitimate criticism, hate speech, and defamation in the digital realm.
• It called for a balanced approach to protect fundamental rights while preventing misuse of digital platforms.
• The ruling has significant implications for social media intermediaries and content creators.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution- historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Fundamental Rights
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court ruled on freedom of speech in digital media.
It affirmed that reasonable restrictions under Article 19(2) apply to online content.
The ruling aims to distinguish between legitimate criticism, hate speech, and defamation.
Article 19(1)(a) guarantees freedom of speech and expression.
mainsPoints: Discuss the complexities of balancing freedom of speech with public order, national security, and individual rights in the digital age.
Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling for content regulation on social media platforms and accountability of intermediaries.
Examine how this judgment contributes to the ongoing debate on digital rights and responsibilities in India.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the judiciary in interpreting constitutional provisions in the context of rapidly evolving technology.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel and law books on a judicial desk, symbolizing a court ruling.
GS2.trivia: The 'reasonable restrictions' under Article 19(2) include grounds like public order, decency, morality, and defamation.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Access to Climate Finance and Technology Transfer at UN General Assembly
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for developed nations to fulfill their commitments on climate finance to developing countries.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for accessible and affordable climate technologies for mitigation and adaptation.
• It highlighted the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
• India underscored its own ambitious climate action targets despite limited historical emissions.
• The discussions focused on accelerating global climate action ahead of the next COP summit.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: India advocated for equitable climate finance and technology transfer at the UNGA.
It emphasized the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC).
Developed nations' commitments on climate finance were a key point.
The discussions are crucial for the upcoming Conference of Parties (COP) summit.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's stance on climate justice and its role in advocating for developing countries' interests in global climate negotiations.
Analyze the challenges in mobilizing and accessing adequate climate finance and technology transfer for developing nations.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international mechanisms and agreements in addressing climate change, considering the principle of CBDR-RC.
Examine India's own climate action initiatives and their alignment with global sustainability goals.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from various countries attending a session at the UN General Assembly.
GS2.trivia: The Paris Agreement (2015) aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius, compared to pre-industrial levels.

GS2.headline: G20 Sherpas Meet to Review Progress on India's Presidency Legacy Projects, Focus on Global South Development
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Sherpas convened to assess the implementation of key initiatives launched during India's presidency in 2023.
• Discussions focused on the 'Voice of Global South Summit' outcomes and strengthening multilateral development banks.
• Progress on digital public infrastructure, climate finance, and sustainable development goals was reviewed.
• India emphasized the need for continued global cooperation to address challenges faced by developing countries.
• The meeting aimed to ensure continuity and impact of India's G20 legacy beyond its immediate term.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: G20 Sherpas met to review progress on India's presidency legacy projects.
Key focus areas included outcomes from the 'Voice of Global South Summit'.
Discussions covered digital public infrastructure and climate finance.
G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's G20 Presidency and its efforts to amplify the voice of the Global South.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring the continuity and effective implementation of G20 legacy projects across different presidencies.
Evaluate the role of multilateral institutions in addressing global challenges such as climate change, debt distress, and sustainable development.
Examine India's growing influence in global governance and its commitment to multilateralism.
GS2.imageDescription: Official logo of India's G20 Presidency with delegates in a meeting.
GS2.trivia: The G20 represents around 85% of global GDP, over 75% of global trade, and about two-thirds of the world population.

GS2.headline: India-Africa Trade Summit Concludes with Focus on Digital Partnerships and Capacity Building
GS2.explainer:
• The annual summit aimed at strengthening economic ties and strategic cooperation between India and African nations.
• Key agreements were signed for digital infrastructure development, fintech collaboration, and skill enhancement programs.
• India reiterated its commitment to supporting Africa's development priorities through concessional credit lines and technical assistance.
• Discussions also covered food security, healthcare, and renewable energy partnerships.
• The summit emphasized a 'South-South Cooperation' model based on mutual respect and shared growth.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: The India-Africa Trade Summit focused on digital partnerships and capacity building.
Agreements were signed for digital infrastructure and fintech collaboration.
India offers concessional credit lines and technical assistance to African nations.
The summit promotes a 'South-South Cooperation' model.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving dynamics of India-Africa relations and the significance of 'South-South Cooperation' in a multipolar world.
Analyze the key areas of economic engagement between India and African nations, particularly in digital and capacity building sectors.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for India in strengthening its strategic and economic footprint across Africa.
Examine how India's development partnership model differs from that of other global powers in Africa.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and African delegates at a conference table with flags of participating nations.
GS2.trivia: India is Africa's third-largest trading partner, and Africa is India's fourth-largest trading partner.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Blue Economy Mission 2.0' to Boost Marine Resource Sustainability and Economic Growth
GS3.explainer:
• The second phase of the mission focuses on advanced marine technology, sustainable fishing practices, and ocean energy.
• It aims to create new economic opportunities for coastal communities and promote responsible tourism.
• Key pillars include deep-sea exploration, marine spatial planning, and climate change adaptation for coastal regions.
• The mission emphasizes scientific research and indigenous technology development for ocean resources.
• It aligns with India's commitments to UN Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water).
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications
prelimsPoints: Blue Economy Mission 2.0 is an initiative by the Government of India.
Its primary goals are sustainable use of marine resources and economic growth.
Focus areas include deep-sea exploration, sustainable fishing, and ocean energy.
It is linked to UN Sustainable Development Goal 14 (Life Below Water).
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the Blue Economy for India's economic development and environmental sustainability.
Discuss the challenges in implementing such a mission, including technological gaps, regulatory hurdles, and stakeholder conflicts.
Evaluate its potential impact on coastal livelihoods, national security, and India's geopolitical standing in the Indian Ocean region.
Suggest measures for effective inter-ministerial coordination and community participation.
GS3.imageDescription: An infographic showing various aspects of the Blue Economy, including fisheries, shipping, and renewable ocean energy.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km and an Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) of more than 2 million sq km.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Global Economic Headwinds and Domestic Inflationary Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach due to persistent global economic uncertainties and elevated core inflation.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the 4% target while supporting growth.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were assessed as adequate, but vigilance remains high.
• Experts suggest a wait-and-watch approach by the central bank before considering any rate cuts.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary Policy
Inflation
prelimsPoints: The Repo Rate was maintained at 6.5% by the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC).
The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
India's inflation target is 4% with a +/- 2% band.
Global economic headwinds and domestic inflationary pressures influenced the decision.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and economic growth in the current global scenario.
Analyze the potential impact of a prolonged status quo on interest rates on investment, consumption, and credit growth.
Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India's monetary policy framework and its effectiveness.
Evaluate how external factors like global crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions influence domestic monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: Graph showing the trend of RBI's repo rate over the past year.
GS3.trivia: The MPC in India consists of six members: three from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite 'EOS-08' for Enhanced Agricultural and Disaster Monitoring
GS3.explainer:
• The EOS-08 satellite was launched using the PSLV-C58 from Sriharikota, marking another milestone for ISRO.
• It is designed to provide high-resolution images for agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster management.
• The satellite features enhanced synthetic aperture radar (SAR) capabilities for all-weather imaging.
• Data from EOS-08 will be crucial for natural resource management and environmental monitoring.
• This mission reinforces India's self-reliance in space technology and applications.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: EOS-08 is an Earth Observation Satellite launched by ISRO.
It was launched using the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58).
Its applications include agricultural mapping, urban planning, and disaster monitoring.
The satellite features Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) for all-weather imaging.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of India's space program in national development, with a focus on Earth observation satellites.
Analyze how advanced remote sensing technologies, like SAR, contribute to disaster management and resource planning.
Examine the potential for commercialization of ISRO's services and its implications for India's space economy.
Evaluate the significance of indigenous space technology in achieving self-reliance and strategic autonomy.
GS3.imageDescription: Launch of PSLV-C58 carrying EOS-08 from Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the few countries capable of launching satellites using its own launch vehicles.

GS3.headline: New Framework for Cyber Security Collaboration Between States and Central Agencies Announced
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs launched a new framework to enhance coordination in combating cybercrime and cyber threats.
• It establishes a unified reporting mechanism and incident response protocols for state and central agencies.
• The framework aims to share intelligence, best practices, and resources more effectively.
• Capacity building for state law enforcement in cyber forensics and investigation is a key focus.
• This initiative is critical to address the rising sophistication and cross-border nature of cyber attacks.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
prelimsPoints: A new framework for cyber security collaboration has been launched by MHA.
It aims to improve coordination between central and state agencies in combating cybercrime.
Key aspects include unified reporting, incident response, and intelligence sharing.
Capacity building for state law enforcement in cyber forensics is a focus.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving landscape of cyber threats in India and the necessity for robust inter-agency coordination.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the new framework in addressing the challenges of cyber security.
Examine the role of technology and human resources in strengthening India's cyber defense capabilities.
Evaluate the implications of cyber security breaches on national security, critical infrastructure, and individual privacy.
GS3.imageDescription: A network security graphic illustrating data protection and threat detection.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries targeted by cyber attacks globally.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to internal security through communication networks

GS3.headline: National Mission on Sustainable Water Management Releases Comprehensive Action Plan for Water Security
GS3.explainer:
• The mission's action plan focuses on integrated water resource management, emphasizing both demand and supply-side interventions.
• Key strategies include promoting water-efficient agriculture, rainwater harvesting, and groundwater recharge.
• It also addresses urban water supply, wastewater treatment, and rejuvenation of traditional water bodies.
• The plan calls for enhanced inter-state cooperation and community participation in water governance.
• Climate change impacts on water resources and adaptation strategies are central to the mission's goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment

Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
prelimsPoints: The National Mission on Sustainable Water Management released its action plan.
It focuses on integrated water resource management.
Strategies include water-efficient agriculture, rainwater harvesting, and groundwater recharge.
It addresses urban water supply and wastewater treatment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted water crisis in India and the need for a comprehensive national action plan.
Analyze the effectiveness of integrated water resource management approaches in addressing both rural and urban water challenges.
Evaluate the role of inter-state cooperation and community participation in ensuring water security.
Examine the impact of climate change on India's water resources and suggest adaptation and mitigation strategies.
GS3.imageDescription: A diagram illustrating different methods of water harvesting and conservation.
GS3.trivia: India holds 18% of the world's population but only 4% of the world's freshwater resources.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Incubation Fund to Boost Agri-Tech Startups and Modernize Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The 'Krishi Navachar Nidhi' (Agricultural Innovation Fund) aims to support startups developing tech solutions for agriculture.
• Focus areas include precision farming, supply chain management, farm mechanization, and market linkages.
• The fund provides financial assistance, mentorship, and access to research facilities for selected startups.
• It seeks to bridge the gap between agricultural research and practical application on the ground.
• This initiative is expected to enhance farmer income, improve productivity, and reduce post-harvest losses.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Issues relating to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The 'Krishi Navachar Nidhi' is a new incubation fund for agri-tech startups.
It supports startups in areas like precision farming and supply chain management.
The fund provides financial assistance and mentorship.
Its objective is to modernize agriculture and enhance farmer income.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of technology and innovation in transforming India's agricultural sector and addressing its challenges.
Analyze how government support for agri-tech startups can contribute to improving farmer incomes and food security.
Evaluate the potential of precision farming and digital agriculture in enhancing productivity and sustainability.
Examine the hurdles faced by agri-tech startups in India and suggest policy measures to foster their growth.
GS3.imageDescription: Farmers using drones for crop monitoring and smart irrigation systems.
GS3.trivia: India's agricultural sector employs more than half of the country's workforce.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Economy
prelimsSyllabus.topic: e-technology in the aid of farmers

GS3.headline: Ministry of Civil Aviation Releases Draft Policy for Urban Air Mobility and Drone-based Logistics
GS3.explainer:
• The draft policy aims to create a regulatory framework for electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
• It outlines guidelines for drone operations in urban areas, including delivery services and passenger transport.
• Safety, security, and air traffic management for urban air mobility are key considerations.
• The policy encourages investment and innovation in the nascent urban air mobility sector.
• It seeks to alleviate urban congestion and enhance last-mile connectivity through advanced aerial solutions.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: The Ministry of Civil Aviation released a draft policy for Urban Air Mobility (UAM).
It includes regulations for eVTOL aircraft and drone-based logistics.
Safety, security, and air traffic management are key focus areas.
The policy aims to reduce urban congestion and improve connectivity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of Urban Air Mobility (UAM) and drone technology to revolutionize transportation and logistics in India.
Analyze the regulatory challenges involved in integrating UAM into existing urban infrastructure and airspace management.
Evaluate the socio-economic benefits and environmental impacts of drone-based services and eVTOL aircraft.
Examine the role of government policy in fostering innovation while ensuring safety and public acceptance of new aerial technologies.
GS3.imageDescription: Concept art of an eVTOL aircraft flying over a futuristic city skyline.
GS3.trivia: India's Drone Rules 2021 liberalized regulations for drone operations in the country.

GS3.headline: National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 Targets Zero Landfill Cities by 2035
GS3.explainer:
• The new policy aims to achieve 100% waste segregation at source and promote circular economy principles.
• It mandates advanced waste processing technologies, including Waste-to-Energy (WtE) plants and composting.
• Financial incentives and penalties are introduced to ensure compliance by urban local bodies and citizens.
• The policy emphasizes public awareness campaigns and private sector investment in waste management infrastructure.
• It is designed to tackle the growing urban waste crisis and improve public health and environmental quality.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and disaster management
GS3.source: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
prelimsPoints: The National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 targets zero landfill cities by 2035.
It mandates 100% waste segregation at source.
The policy promotes Waste-to-Energy (WtE) plants and composting.
Financial incentives and penalties are part of the policy framework.
mainsPoints: Discuss the magnitude of India's urban waste crisis and its environmental and public health implications.
Analyze the key features of the National Urban Waste Management Policy 2025 and its potential for transforming waste management practices.
Evaluate the challenges in achieving zero landfill cities, including behavioral change, technological adoption, and financial viability.
Examine the role of local governments, private sector, and citizen participation in successful policy implementation.
GS3.imageDescription: A modern waste processing facility with waste segregation and recycling operations.
GS3.trivia: India generates over 1.5 lakh metric tonnes of municipal solid waste every day.

GS3.headline: India's Renewable Energy Progress Report Shows Significant Strides Towards 2030 Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The report highlights a substantial increase in solar and wind power generation capacity over the last year.
• It notes accelerated deployment of utility-scale renewable projects and decentralized solutions.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, storage solutions, and land acquisition for large projects.
• India is on track to achieve its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• The report calls for continued policy support and private investment to meet ambitious 2030 targets.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
GS3.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy capacity has seen significant growth.
The country is progressing towards its 2030 Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
Challenges include grid integration and energy storage solutions.
Solar and wind power are major contributors to renewable energy generation.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's journey towards achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets and its contribution to global climate action.
Analyze the key drivers and challenges in accelerating the deployment of renewable energy technologies in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies, private investment, and technological innovation in the renewable energy sector.
Examine the implications of a growing renewable energy share on India's energy security and economic development.
GS3.imageDescription: A vast solar power farm stretching across a landscape under a clear sky.
GS3.trivia: India has the world's fourth-largest installed renewable energy capacity.
